text.title: aws-vpc-three-tier Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for aws-vpc-three-tier. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Production VPCs MUST span at least 3 Availability Zones with 3 subnet tiers each (public, private, database) totaling 9 subnets. CIDR blocks MUST be planned to avoid overlaps with other VPCs to permit future Transit Gateway or VPC peering. Enable DNS hostnames and DNS support on every VPC.
rule.rationale: 3 AZs provides N+2 redundancy. Public/private/database isolation is required by PCI-DSS and SOC 2. Non-overlapping CIDRs are a hard requirement for Transit Gateway routing; retrofitting is expensive.

rule.statement: Production environments MUST deploy one NAT gateway per Availability Zone (one_nat_gateway_per_az=true). Dev/staging MAY use a single NAT gateway to reduce cost. NAT gateways MUST be deployed in public subnets only. Each private subnet's route table MUST point to the NAT gateway in the same AZ.
rule.rationale: A single NAT gateway is a single point of failure and routes all AZ cross-traffic through one AZ (incurring data transfer charges). Per-AZ NAT gateways cost ~$32/month each but eliminate the cross-AZ charge and the SPOF.

rule.statement: All VPCs MUST have a Gateway endpoint for S3 (free) attached to all private route tables. Interface endpoints for ECR (ecr.api + ecr.dkr) and Secrets Manager MUST be created in private subnets when containers or instances pull images or fetch secrets — this eliminates NAT gateway charges for those traffic flows and removes the public internet from the path.
rule.rationale: S3 Gateway endpoint is free and eliminates S3 traffic from NAT gateway bandwidth billing. ECR interface endpoints save ~$0.09/GB vs NAT for image pulls. Secrets Manager via interface endpoint keeps credentials fetches entirely private.

rule.statement: Application Load Balancers serving production traffic MUST: be internet-facing with HTTPS listener using TLS 1.2+ (ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06), have an HTTP listener that redirects to HTTPS, and use ACM-managed certificates. VPC Flow Logs MUST be enabled on all production VPCs, writing to CloudWatch Logs with 30-day retention minimum.
rule.rationale: HTTP-only ALBs transmit credentials and session tokens in cleartext. TLS policy enforcement prevents TLS 1.0/1.1 downgrade attacks. Flow logs are mandatory for incident investigation and compliance evidence.
